reasoning: The stock recently experienced a massive ~30% run-up in July and August on the back of strong Q2 earnings (driven by AI fiber-optics demand) and raised Q3 guidance. It currently sits at $66.68, slightly higher than the highest Wall Street price targets for the year (which were around $65) and significantly above the average target ($56-57). In the near term (1 week to 1 month), this creates a high likelihood of consolidation or mean-reversion as the market digests the rally, supported by the Yfinance statistical projection of P(increase) = 0.43 for the 1-week horizon. However, over the medium-to-long term (3 to 6 months), the underlying AI structural demand, combined with the general market's upward drift, will likely lead to further price target upgrades from analysts after Q3 earnings. Therefore, the probabilities gradually revert to >0.5 as the forecast horizon extends into 2026.
